english: Marinated Chicken and Button Mushroom Pizza

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572); no specific chicken-and-mushroom pizza subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category; no specific pollo e funghi variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chicken and mushroom pizza. Generic pizza food concept not confirmed in SNOMED CT release; null used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is a medium-sized pizza of approximately 300 g, equivalent to roughly two to three slices as served at an Italian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese, with meat and vegetables — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299 / FDC ID 173960)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for pollo e funghi pizza exists. Values adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for pizza with cheese, meat, and vegetables (FDC ID 173960), which closely matches the macronutrient profile of a thin-crust Italian pizza topped with chicken, mushrooms, tomato sauce, and mozzarella. Sodium and saturated fat values cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods pizza entries. Values represent a composite estimate appropriate for a restaurant-style Italian chicken and mushroom pizza.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pollo e funghi pizza specifically. GI of 50 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which report GI values for pizza in the range of 45–60 depending on crust thickness and toppings. A thin-crust pizza with protein-rich toppings (chicken, cheese) and moderate fat content typically falls at the lower end of this range. GI of 50 selected as a defensible central estimate for a thin-crust Italian pizza with meat and vegetable toppings. GL per serving calculated as (50 × 26.5 × 300 / 100) / 100 = 39.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1560 mg per full serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one or two slices and avoid adding extra salt or high-sodium condiments, as a full serving can contribute approximately 65–78% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) and significant carbohydrate load per serving (~79.5 g per 300 g serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion to one or two slices, pair with a non-starchy vegetable side, and monitor postprandial glucose, as the moderate GI combined with a large carbohydrate load per full serving can produce a meaningful glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g per 100 g) and sodium (~520 mg per 100 g) from cheese and processed dough
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake to one or two slices per occasion, choose thin-crust variants, and be mindful of cumulative saturated fat and sodium intake, as a full serving provides approximately 11.4 g saturated fat, approaching the recommended daily limit of 20 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pollo e Funghi Pizza is a classic Italian pizza topped with moist marinated chicken breast and button mushrooms, typically finished with mozzarella cheese, tomato sauce, and fresh herbs on a thin or medium-thickness baked wheat dough base. The name translates from Italian as 'chicken and mushroom pizza.' The chicken is usually marinated in olive oil, garlic, and herbs before being placed on the pizza, giving it a tender, flavourful character, while the button mushrooms add an earthy umami depth. The pizza is baked in a high-temperature oven, producing a lightly crisp crust with a soft, chewy interior. Nutritionally, it provides a moderate energy density of approximately 230 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein contribution (~12.5 g per 100 g) from both the chicken and cheese, making it more satiating than vegetarian pizza variants. The fat content (~8.5 g per 100 g) is moderate, with saturated fat primarily from mozzarella. Carbohydrate content (~26.5 g per 100 g) comes predominantly from the wheat dough base. Sodium is elevated (~520 mg per 100 g) due to cheese, tomato sauce, and seasoning, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full serving is substantial due to the large carbohydrate mass. This dish is widely served in Italian restaurants across Singapore, including at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore. It is suitable for general consumption but requires portion awareness for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.

Calories in chicken and mushroom pizza

ingredient_synonyms.pizza dough: pizza base, pizza crust, wheat dough
ingredient_synonyms.tomato sauce: pizza sauce, pomodoro sauce, tomato base

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for pollo e funghi pizza exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy entry for pizza with cheese, meat, and vegetables (FDC ID 173960), cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods pizza data. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables using analogous thin-crust meat pizza data. Confidence is medium because the adaptation is methodologically sound but not an exact match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for this record. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for this record. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for this record. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, and zinc values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 173960 and are approximate. This record was sourced from field survey data (Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore - Boat Quay); the restaurant-specific recipe may differ from the USDA-adapted values used here. Price data (SGD 29.32) is preserved from the input record for reference. The food is not a Singaporean dish by cuisine origin (Italian), so Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are not applicable and have been left null; Chinese names are provided as supplementary translations for Singapore market context. GI category is low (GI = 50, which is ≤ 55 per the Veritas threshold rule).
